title: Eranthemum aciculare
taxonomy: Eranthemum aciculare belongs to the family Lamiaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and is also known as the "needle-leaf lantana". The classification of Eranthemum aciculare further breaks down into the genus Eranthemum and the subfamily Lamioideae. Eranthemum aciculare is a species of flowering plant, and its classification also includes the kingdom Plantae. This species is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive flowers and compact growth habit.

intro: Eranthemum aciculare, also known as the "Stick Skeleton Plant", is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Eranthemum aciculare include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the form of extracts and tinctures.
